topic.what: Virtualization-based defense framework protecting LLM agents against direct and indirect prompt injection attacks
topic.how: Enforces semantic privilege separation via STI (Suitability, Taint, Integrity) audit protocol with one-shot self-correction mechanism that intercepts tool calls through a trusted semantic visor
topic.why_matters: Current defenses lack principled security architecture separating trusted control from untrusted model behavior, leading to trade-offs between security and utility; AgentVisor provides systematic mitigation without compromising functionality

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of cases where agent executes injected malicious task
characteristics.context: Achieves perfect 0.00% ASR on direct injection across all attack vectors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Task completion rate when attack is present
characteristics.evidence: Tables 1-2 showing UA across attack scenarios
characteristics.context: Maintains high utility across attack conditions unlike detection-based methods that over-defend

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance loss vs no-defense baseline on benign tasks
characteristics.context: Minimal utility loss while achieving strong security

characteristics.what_itasures: Inference time increase compared to no defense
characteristics.context: Benign: 1.4×; Attack: up to 2.32× due to self-correction

characteristics.value: Implied low; UA 85-91% on attacks indicates minimal over-blocking
characteristics.context: Self-correction mechanism prevents utility collapse seen in blocking-only defenses

characteristics.what_it_measures: Visor accuracy in identifying injection attempts
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3 showing GPT-4o and GLM-4.7 detection
characteristics.context: Target agents can recognize injections when explicitly asked but need enforcement

novelty: virtualization-inspired privilege separation for LLM agents
STI protocol translating OS security primitives to semantic audit
one-shot self-correction mechanism replacing brittle blocking
semantic exception injection analogous to OS interrupt handling
context isolation between Guest and Visor preventing information leakage
open_problems: scalability to extremely long conversations with context window constraints
multimodal generalization for visual/audio injection attacks
computational overhead optimization for resource-constrained scenarios
